key_insight: The paper proves that adaptive FEM can achieve optimal complexity even when driven by non-residual error estimators, provided the estimator and iterative solver satisfy abstract assumptions.
engineering_relevance: This supports practical, efficient simulation workflows for symmetric elliptic PDEs by allowing adaptive mesh refinement and algebraic solves to converge robustly without relying on residual-based indicators.
method: Formulate adaptive FEM with coupled mesh refinement and iterative linear solving.
Assume abstract properties for the a-posteriori estimator and solver.
Prove unconditional full R-linear convergence, and optimal complexity for sufficiently small adaptivity parameters.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports theoretical convergence and complexity guarantees, measured against standard nonlinear approximation classes and total computational cost, but does not mention numerical experiments.
watch_item: The optimality guarantee depends on sufficiently small user-chosen adaptivity parameters and on abstract estimator/solver assumptions, so implementation must verify these conditions carefully.
paper_title: Optimal complexity of adaptive FEM for second-order linear elliptic PDEs driven by non-residual estimators, Part I: Symmetric PDEs
